fixSteps.instruction: On the printer display or HP Smart app, check the estimated ink levels for each cartridge to determine which needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Continue Printing
fixSteps.instruction: You can continue printing with a low ink warning. The printer will stop when a cartridge is completely empty.

fixSteps.title: Order Replacement Cartridges
fixSteps.instruction: Order replacement cartridges for the low color(s). Check the label inside the cartridge door for correct cartridge numbers.

fixSteps.title: Replace When Ready
fixSteps.instruction: When prints become faded or streaky, replace the cartridge. Open the cartridge door, pull out the old cartridge, remove tape from the new one, and insert firmly.

whenToCallPro: No professional service needed. This is a normal consumable replacement.

faq.q: How accurate are ink level estimates?
faq.a: Ink level estimates are approximate. The printer estimates based on pages printed and droplets fired, not by directly measuring ink volume. Actual remaining ink may vary.

faq.q: Can I override the low ink warning?
faq.a: Yes, the printer will continue printing with a low ink warning. You may need to press OK or Continue if the printer pauses. The printer stops only when ink is estimated to be empty.

faq.q: Should I subscribe to HP Instant Ink?
faq.a: HP Instant Ink is a subscription service that automatically ships cartridges when ink is low. It can save money for moderate to heavy print users, with plans starting at a few dollars per month.

title: Ink Cartridge Empty
shortDescription: One or more ink cartridges are completely empty.
fullDescription: Error E9 indicates one or more ink cartridges are estimated to be empty and must be replaced before the printer can continue. The printer blocks printing to prevent printhead damage from dry firing.

causes.cause: Ink completely consumed through normal use
Cartridge installed with very low ink remaining
Ink level sensor malfunction indicating empty prematurely

symptoms: E9 error code on display
Printing completely stopped
Specific color cartridge shown as empty
Replace cartridge prompt on screen

fixSteps.title: Identify Empty Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Check the printer display or HP Smart app to see which cartridge needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cartridge access door, remove the empty cartridge by pressing down on it, remove the tape from the new cartridge, and insert it into the correct slot until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Run Alignment
fixSteps.instruction: After installing a new cartridge, the printer may prompt for an alignment page. Follow the on-screen instructions to align the printhead.

fixSteps.title: Print Test Page
fixSteps.instruction: Print a test page to verify the new cartridge is functioning properly and colors are correct.

whenToCallPro: No professional service needed.

faq.q: Can I print in black if only a color cartridge is empty?
faq.a: Some HP printers allow single-cartridge mode for black-only printing when color cartridges are empty. Check your printer's documentation for this feature.

faq.q: Can I refill my cartridge instead of replacing it?
faq.a: Refill kits are available but HP does not recommend them. Refilled cartridges may not have accurate ink level reporting and could leak, causing damage.

faq.q: How many pages does a cartridge typically print?
faq.a: Standard cartridges print 100-200 pages. High-yield (XL) cartridges print 300-600 pages. Actual yield depends on coverage, print settings, and content type.

title: Printer Not Responding Error
shortDescription: The computer cannot communicate with the printer for installation.
fullDescription: Error 0xc4eb8482 typically appears during HP printer software installation on Windows when the installer cannot establish communication with the printer. This is related to driver installation, USB enumeration, or network discovery failures.

causes.cause: USB cable not connected or faulty during installation
Printer not in setup mode for wireless installation

symptoms: Installation wizard fails with 0xc4eb8482
Printer not found during setup
Setup progress stalls at connection step
Error appears at the end of a seemingly successful install

fixSteps.title: Verify Connection
fixSteps.instruction: For USB: try a different USB cable and port. For wireless: ensure the printer is in setup mode (usually indicated by a flashing Wi-Fi light).

fixSteps.title: Temporarily Disable Firewall
fixSteps.instruction: Temporarily disable Windows Firewall and any third-party security software during installation. Re-enable after installation is complete.

fixSteps.title: Use HP Smart App
fixSteps.instruction: Instead of the traditional installer, try using the HP Smart app from the Microsoft Store to set up the printer. It often handles connection issues better.

fixSteps.title: Clean Install
fixSteps.instruction: Uninstall all HP software, clear the printer from Devices and Printers, restart the computer, and run a fresh download of the installer from support.hp.com.

whenToCallPro: If repeated installation attempts fail across different methods, there may be a deeper Windows issue or a hardware fault with the printer's USB or network interface.

faq.q: Should I use the CD that came with the printer?
faq.a: No, always download the latest software from support.hp.com. The CD may contain outdated drivers that do not work well with current Windows versions.

faq.q: Can I use the printer without HP software?
faq.a: Yes, Windows has built-in print support. Add the printer through Settings > Printers & Scanners. You may lose advanced features like scanning and ink management but basic printing will work.

faq.q: Why does the installer not find my wireless printer?
faq.a: The printer may not be in setup mode. Reset the wireless settings on the printer (usually by pressing and holding the wireless button for 5+ seconds) and try again.

title: Supply Memory Error
shortDescription: The printer cannot read the memory chip on a toner cartridge.
fullDescription: Error 10.XX.YY indicates the printer cannot communicate with the memory chip on one or more toner cartridges. The XX identifies the cartridge color/position and YY indicates the specific fault. These chips store ink level data, cartridge identification, and usage tracking information.

causes.cause: Dirty or damaged cartridge memory contacts

symptoms: 10.XX.YY error on control panel
Specific cartridge color flagged
Supply error or consumable error message
Printer refuses to print or prints with reduced quality

fixSteps.title: Reseat the Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cartridge door, remove the identified cartridge (XX identifies which one), and reseat it firmly.

fixSteps.title: Clean Memory Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the cartridge and clean the gold or silver memory contacts on the cartridge with a lint-free cloth. Also clean the matching contacts inside the printer.

fixSteps.title: Try a Different Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Install a different cartridge (new or from a known-working printer) to determine if the issue is the cartridge or the printer.

fixSteps.title: Override Supply Error
fixSteps.instruction: Some HP printers allow you to override supply errors and continue printing. Check the control panel for an Override option or press OK to continue with the error.

whenToCallPro: If multiple cartridges show the error, the printer's memory reader may be faulty, requiring professional service.

faq.q: What do the XX and YY numbers mean?
faq.a: XX identifies the cartridge position: 00=black, 01=cyan, 02=magenta, 03=yellow on color printers. YY identifies the specific memory fault type.

faq.q: Can I still print with this error?
faq.a: On some models, pressing OK or selecting Continue/Override allows printing. The printer may show reduced functionality or disable supply level monitoring.

faq.q: Does using non-HP cartridges cause this error?
faq.a: Third-party cartridges with defective or incompatible memory chips frequently trigger 10.XX.YY errors. Try an HP-original cartridge to diagnose.

title: Printer Door or Cover Open
shortDescription: A printer door or cover is not fully closed.
fullDescription: The Door Open error indicates one of the printer's access doors or covers is not fully latched. The printer has interlock switches that prevent operation when doors are open for user safety. All doors must be fully closed before printing can resume.

causes.cause: Front cover not fully latched after cartridge change
Rear access panel not properly closed
Toner cartridge or imaging drum preventing door closure

symptoms: Door Open message on display
Printer will not start print job
Status light indicating door is open
All functions halted

fixSteps.title: Close All Doors
fixSteps.instruction: Check and firmly close all printer doors: front cover, top cover, rear access panel, and any tray doors. Push until you hear a click.

fixSteps.title: Check Cartridge Seating
fixSteps.instruction: If the front door will not close fully, open it and check that all cartridges and the imaging drum are properly seated.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Door Latches
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the latch mechanism on each door is not broken or obstructed. Clear any debris from the latch area.

fixSteps.title: Check Interlock Switch
fixSteps.instruction: If all doors are physically closed but the error persists, the interlock switch may be stuck or broken. Power cycle the printer first. If the error continues, the switch needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: Only if the interlock switch is defective and the error persists despite all doors being physically closed.

faq.q: Which door is open?
faq.a: On newer HP printers, the display or HP Smart app may indicate which specific door or cover is open. If not, check all access points: front cover, top cover, rear panel, and side panels.

faq.q: Can a broken latch cause this?
faq.a: Yes, if a latch is broken, the door may appear closed but the interlock switch is not engaged. The latch mechanism may need repair or replacement.

faq.q: Is it safe to open the printer while it is on?
faq.a: Yes for normal access doors. The interlock switches stop all mechanical movement and disable the laser when doors are opened, ensuring safety.

title: Toner Cartridge End of Life
shortDescription: A toner cartridge has reached its end of life and must be replaced.
fullDescription: The Replace Cartridge message indicates a toner cartridge is completely depleted or has reached its rated end of life. Unlike a low toner warning, this message means the printer has stopped printing and requires cartridge replacement to continue.

causes.cause: Toner completely consumed through normal printing
Cartridge has reached rated page count regardless of toner remaining
Cartridge counter chip indicates end of life

symptoms: Replace Cartridge message on display
Printing halted completely
Specific cartridge color identified for replacement
Print quality severely degraded before error

fixSteps.title: Identify the Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Check the printer display for which cartridge needs replacement (black, cyan, magenta, or yellow).

fixSteps.title: Remove Old Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover, locate the depleted cartridge, and pull it straight out. Dispose of or recycle properly.

fixSteps.title: Install New Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Unbox the new cartridge, remove the protective strip and any packaging material. Gently rock the cartridge side to side to distribute toner evenly. Insert into the correct slot until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Close Cover and Test
fixSteps.instruction: Close the front cover. The printer will initialize the new cartridge. Print a test page to verify correct installation.

whenToCallPro: Cartridge replacement is a basic user task. Professional service is only needed if the printer does not recognize new cartridges.

faq.q: Can I continue printing with a replace cartridge message?
faq.a: HP printers typically stop printing when a cartridge reaches end of life. Some models have an Override option that allows limited continued use at the risk of poor quality.

faq.q: How do I recycle old cartridges?
faq.a: HP offers a free cartridge recycling program. Return used cartridges via HP Planet Partners. Many office supply stores also accept cartridges for recycling.

faq.q: Should I buy standard or high-yield cartridges?
faq.a: High-yield (X or XL) cartridges cost more upfront but have a lower cost per page. They are more economical for moderate to heavy print volumes.

title: Maintenance Kit End of Life
shortDescription: The printer's maintenance kit has reached its rated page count and should be replaced.
fullDescription: The Perform Printer Maintenance message indicates the printer's maintenance kit components (fuser, transfer roller, pickup rollers, separation pads) have reached their rated page count. While the printer may continue operating, component wear can lead to paper jams and print quality issues.

causes.cause: Printer has reached the maintenance kit rated page count
Maintenance counter triggered by page count timer
Harsh paper types wearing components faster

symptoms: Perform Maintenance message on display
Increasing frequency of paper jams
Print quality degradation (faded prints, toner smearing)
Strange noises from roller or fuser areas

fixSteps.title: Order Maintenance Kit
fixSteps.instruction: Determine your printer model and order the correct maintenance kit from HP or an authorized dealer. The kit number is typically listed in the printer's user guide.

fixSteps.title: Replace Fuser
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear door, release the fuser retention levers, slide out the old fuser, and install the new one. Ensure it is firmly locked in place.

fixSteps.title: Replace Rollers and Separation Pads
fixSteps.instruction: Following the instructions included with the maintenance kit, replace the pickup rollers and separation pads in each tray.

fixSteps.title: Reset Maintenance Counter
fixSteps.instruction: After replacing all components, reset the maintenance counter from the printer's service menu. This varies by model but typically involves navigating to Administration > Manage Supplies > Reset Maintenance Kit.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable replacing the fuser and rollers, a service technician can perform the maintenance kit installation.

faq.q: Can I ignore the maintenance message?
faq.a: You can continue printing, but expect increasing paper jams and declining print quality. The maintenance kit components are wearing out and will eventually fail.

faq.q: How often is maintenance needed?
faq.a: Maintenance kits are rated for 150,000-350,000 pages depending on the model. In a typical office printing 2,000 pages per month, that is 6-15 years.

faq.q: What is included in a maintenance kit?
faq.a: A typical kit includes a new fuser, transfer roller, pickup rollers for each tray, and separation pads. Some kits may also include a transfer belt or other model-specific components.

title: Printer Offline Status
shortDescription: The printer appears as offline on the computer and cannot receive print jobs.
fullDescription: Printer Offline is a status message from the operating system indicating it cannot communicate with the printer. The printer itself may be functioning normally but the computer has lost connectivity to it. This is one of the most common printer issues and can be caused by network, driver, or system settings.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi connection lost between printer and router
Printer set to Use Printer Offline mode in Windows
Print spooler service stopped or crashed
USB cable disconnected or port failure

symptoms: Printer shows Offline in Windows Devices and Printers
Print jobs stuck in queue and not printing
HP Smart app shows printer as unavailable
Printer responds to control panel but not from computer

fixSteps.title: Uncheck Use Printer Offline
fixSteps.instruction: Open Control Panel > Devices and Printers, right-click your HP printer, and if 'Use Printer Offline' is checked, click it to uncheck it.

fixSteps.title: Restart Print Spooler
fixSteps.instruction: Open Services (services.msc), find Print Spooler, right-click and select Restart.

fixSteps.title: Verify Network Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Print a network configuration page from the printer. Verify the IP address and try pinging it from your computer. If wireless, confirm the printer is connected to the correct Wi-Fi network.

fixSteps.title: Run HP Print and Scan Doctor
fixSteps.instruction: Download and run HP Print and Scan Doctor. It automatically detects and fixes most offline issues.

fixSteps.title: Remove and Re-add Printer
fixSteps.instruction: If nothing else works, remove the printer from Devices and Printers and add it again. For wireless printers, use the IP address or HP Smart to add it.

whenToCallPro: If the printer's network hardware (Wi-Fi module or Ethernet port) has failed, professional service or replacement may be needed.

faq.q: Why does my printer keep going offline?
faq.a: The most common cause is Wi-Fi instability. Assign a static IP address to the printer and ensure it is within good range of the router. Also check that your computer is on the same network.

faq.q: How do I prevent the computer from setting the printer offline?
faq.a: In Devices and Printers, right-click the printer, select Printer Properties > Ports, and uncheck 'Enable bidirectional support' if it keeps going offline. Also ensure SNMP settings are correct.

faq.q: Can I use HP Smart to fix offline issues?
faq.a: Yes, the HP Smart app includes diagnostic tools that can automatically detect and fix many connectivity issues. It is available from the Microsoft Store or Apple App Store.

title: Fax Communication Error
shortDescription: The fax transmission failed due to a communication error.
fullDescription: Fax Error 344 indicates the fax handshake between the sending and receiving fax machines failed. The machines could not agree on compatible communication parameters (speed, compression, error correction). This is a common fax error in environments with poor line quality or VoIP connections.

causes.cause: Poor phone line quality or noise
Fax speed set too high for the line
Receiving fax machine error or incompatibility

symptoms: Fax error 344 in the fax log
Fax transmission fails after partial page
Repeated failed fax attempts to same number
Fax connects but drops during page transmission

fixSteps.title: Reduce Fax Speed
fixSteps.instruction: On the printer control panel, go to Fax Settings > Advanced > Fax Speed and reduce from Fast (33600) to Medium (14400) or Slow (9600).

fixSteps.title: Enable Error Correction
fixSteps.instruction: In Fax Settings, ensure Error Correction Mode (ECM) is enabled. ECM helps compensate for noisy phone lines.

fixSteps.title: Check Phone Line
fixSteps.instruction: Connect a regular phone handset to the line and listen for static or noise. If the line is noisy, contact your phone provider.

fixSteps.title: Try a Different Fax Method
fixSteps.instruction: If using VoIP, fax reliability may be inherently poor. Consider using an internet fax service instead of analog faxing over VoIP.

affectedSeries: HP Color LaserJet Pro MFP M479
whenToCallPro: If fax errors persist on a good analog phone line, the printer's fax modem may be faulty.

faq.q: Does VoIP affect fax reliability?
faq.a: Yes, significantly. VoIP compresses audio in ways that are incompatible with fax tones. Many VoIP providers offer a special fax mode or T.38 protocol. Alternatively, use an internet fax service.

faq.q: What fax speed should I use?
faq.a: Start with Slow (9600) for maximum reliability. If that works, you can try Medium (14400). Fast (33600) only works reliably on high-quality analog phone lines.

faq.q: Can I see a fax error log?
faq.a: Yes, go to Fax Settings > Fax Reports > Fax Log. This shows all sent and received faxes with status codes and helps identify recurring issues.

title: Fax Busy Signal / No Answer
shortDescription: The receiving fax number returned a busy signal or did not answer.
fullDescription: Fax Error 345 indicates the printer attempted to send a fax but the receiving number was busy or did not answer within the configured timeout. This is a normal operational condition when the receiving machine is occupied or the wrong number was dialed.

causes.cause: Receiving fax machine busy with another fax
Receiving machine powered off or disconnected
Dial prefix required but not configured

symptoms: Fax error 345 in fax log
Fax reports busy signal
Fax fails after ring timeout
Multiple retries all fail

fixSteps.title: Verify Fax Number
fixSteps.instruction: Double-check the fax number. Ensure you include any required area codes or country codes. Confirm the number is a fax line and not a voice line.

fixSteps.title: Wait and Retry
fixSteps.instruction: Wait 5-10 minutes and try again. The receiving machine may have been busy with another fax. The printer may also auto-retry based on its settings.

fixSteps.title: Configure Dial Prefix
fixSteps.instruction: If your phone system requires dialing 9 or 0 for an outside line, configure the dial prefix in Fax Settings > Fax Setup > Dialing Prefix.

fixSteps.title: Adjust Retry Settings
fixSteps.instruction: In Fax Settings, increase the number of redial attempts and the interval between retries for busy numbers.

affectedSeries: HP Color LaserJet Pro MFP M479
whenToCallPro: This is a normal operational condition and does not require professional service.

faq.q: How many times will my printer retry?
faq.a: By default, most HP printers retry 3-5 times with 5-minute intervals. You can adjust these settings in Fax Settings > Fax Setup.

faq.q: Can I schedule a fax for later?
faq.a: Yes, most HP MFP printers support scheduled fax sending. Use the Fax Settings menu to set a future date and time for transmission.

faq.q: How do I know if my fax went through?
faq.a: Check the Fax Log on the printer. A successful fax shows a 'Sent' or 'OK' status. You can also configure the printer to print a confirmation report after each fax.

title: Paper Jam in Output Bin Area
shortDescription: Paper is jammed in the output bin or exit area of the printer.
fullDescription: A jam in the output bin indicates paper has stalled after exiting the fuser but before being fully delivered to the output tray. The paper is typically visible and partially protruding from the output slot. This can be caused by excessive curl from the fuser, worn exit rollers, or an overloaded output bin.

causes.cause: Output bin overfilled with printed pages
Paper catching on output bin edge or guide

symptoms: Jam in output bin error message
Paper visible sticking out of output slot
Last page of print job partially ejected
Paper wrinkled or curled in output area

fixSteps.title: Remove Jammed Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Gently pull the jammed paper straight out from the output area. Do not pull backwards into the printer.

fixSteps.title: Clear Output Bin
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all printed pages from the output bin. Do not let output stack above the maximum capacity.

fixSteps.title: Check for Paper Fragments
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear access door and check near the exit rollers for any torn paper pieces.

fixSteps.title: Reduce Curl
fixSteps.instruction: If paper consistently curls at the output, try different paper, enable the 'Reduce Curl' setting in the print driver, or flip the paper stack in the tray to print on the opposite side.

whenToCallPro: Only if the exit rollers are physically damaged or the output mechanism is broken.

faq.q: Why does my paper curl so much?
faq.a: Paper curl is caused by heat and moisture from the fusing process. It is worse in humid environments and with thin paper. Storing paper in a dry location and using 80+ g/m2 paper reduces curl.

faq.q: How many pages can the output bin hold?
faq.a: Most HP LaserJet output bins hold 150-250 sheets. Check your model's specifications. Do not exceed the capacity or jams will occur.

faq.q: Can I add a paper stopper to the output bin?
faq.a: Some models include an extendable output tray stopper. Pull it out to prevent paper from sliding off the output tray. Aftermarket solutions are also available.

title: Paper Jam in Duplex (Two-Sided) Unit
shortDescription: Paper is jammed in the automatic duplexer during two-sided printing.
fullDescription: A duplex jam occurs when paper is stuck in the automatic duplexing unit that enables two-sided printing. The paper reverses direction to print the second side and can jam during this reversal. This is common with curled, thin, or overloaded paper.

causes.cause: Paper too thin or curled for duplexing
Foreign object in duplex paper path

symptoms: Duplex jam error on display
Paper visible in the rear duplex area
Jams occur only during two-sided printing
First side prints fine but jam occurs during reversal

fixSteps.title: Open Duplex Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Pull out the duplex unit (usually accessible from the rear of the printer). Remove any jammed paper from the unit and the printer cavity.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Duplex Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Push the duplex unit back in firmly until it clicks into place. A loose unit causes misalignment and jams.

fixSteps.title: Use Compatible Paper
fixSteps.instruction: For duplex printing, use paper between 60-120 g/m2. Avoid thin, damp, or curled paper. Use the paper straight from the packaging.

fixSteps.title: Clean Duplex Rollers
fixSteps.instruction: With the duplex unit removed, clean the rollers inside both the unit and the printer with a damp lint-free cloth.

whenToCallPro: If the duplex mechanism is mechanically damaged or the duplex rollers are worn and cannot be cleaned.

faq.q: Can I disable duplexing to avoid jams?
faq.a: Yes, in the print driver settings, change from Two-Sided to One-Sided printing. This avoids the duplex unit entirely.

faq.q: Does my printer have automatic duplexing?
faq.a: Models with 'd', 'dn', or 'dw' in the name typically include automatic duplexing. Check your printer's specifications to confirm.

faq.q: Why does duplexing jam more than single-sided printing?
faq.a: The paper must reverse direction and re-enter the printer for the second side. This additional handling, combined with the paper being warm from the first fusing pass, increases jam potential.

title: Cartridge Fan Error
shortDescription: The toner cartridge area fan has failed or is running abnormally.
fullDescription: Error 57.03 identifies a failure in the fan that cools the toner cartridge area. This fan prevents toner from overheating, which can degrade print quality and damage cartridge components. Color laser printers are especially sensitive to toner temperature control.

causes.cause: Toner dust buildup blocking fan blades

symptoms: 57.03 error on control panel
Toner cartridge area feels unusually warm
Print quality issues with color banding
No airflow detected near cartridge area

fixSteps.title: Clean Ventilation Area
fixSteps.instruction: Power off the printer and use compressed air to clean the ventilation openings near the cartridge area.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off for 60 seconds and restart. A temporary fan stall may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check Fan Internally
fixSteps.instruction: Open the printer and locate the cartridge area fan. Check for obstructions and verify the cable is connected.

fixSteps.title: Replace Fan
fixSteps.instruction: If the fan is confirmed failed, order a replacement and install it.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable opening the printer or if the fan circuit on the DC controller is faulty.

faq.q: Is 57.03 more common on color printers?
faq.a: Yes, color printers have more fans due to the four toner cartridges generating more heat. The cartridge cooling fan is critical for maintaining toner quality.

faq.q: Can overheated toner damage the printer?
faq.a: Yes, overheated toner can clump in the cartridge, cause streaks on prints, and in extreme cases, contaminate the imaging drum and fuser.

faq.q: How can I tell if it is a fan or DC controller issue?
faq.a: If you can hear the fan struggling (grinding, intermittent), it is likely the fan. If there is no sound at all and the cable is connected, it could be either the fan motor or the DC controller circuit.

title: Formatter Fan Error
shortDescription: The formatter board cooling fan has failed.
fullDescription: Error 57.04 indicates the fan that cools the formatter board has failed. The formatter generates significant heat during operation, especially during complex print jobs. Without cooling, the formatter can overheat and cause firmware crashes or hardware damage.

causes.cause: DC controller fan driver circuit failure

symptoms: 57.04 error displayed
Formatter area hot to touch
Random 49 errors from formatter overheating
Printer shutting down during heavy use

fixSteps.title: Clean Formatter Area Vents
fixSteps.instruction: Power off and clean the ventilation grilles near the formatter (typically rear or side of printer) with compressed air.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off, unplug for 60 seconds, reconnect. Allow the printer to cool before restarting.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Formatter Fan
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the formatter access panel (rear of printer) and check that the fan is present, connected, and free of obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Replace Formatter Fan
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the old fan, remove mounting hardware, install the new fan, and reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If the fan circuit on the DC controller has failed, the board needs professional diagnosis and repair.

faq.q: Can formatter overheating cause 49 errors?
faq.a: Yes, formatter overheating due to a failed cooling fan can cause intermittent 49 errors as the firmware crashes from thermal stress.

faq.q: Where is the formatter fan located?
faq.a: On most HP LaserJet Enterprise printers, the formatter and its fan are accessible from a panel on the rear of the printer. The fan blows air across the formatter board.

faq.q: Is the formatter fan the same as the main cooling fan?
faq.a: No, they are separate fans. The main cooling fan (57.01) cools the print engine, while the formatter fan (57.04) specifically cools the main logic board.

title: Main Motor Startup Error
shortDescription: The main motor failed to start within the expected time.
fullDescription: Error 59.01 is a specific variant of the main motor error indicating the motor did not achieve startup speed within the timeout period. This differs from 59.00 (general motor error) in that the motor attempted to start but was too slow.

causes.cause: Mechanical resistance from jammed paper or debris
Low voltage to motor from power supply

symptoms: 59.01 error at startup or during print initiation
Motor makes slow grinding sound
Printer attempts to start but fails
Error may be intermittent in cold environments

fixSteps.title: Remove All Paper and Clear Path
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all paper from trays and carefully check the entire paper path for jammed paper, debris, or foreign objects that could resist motor rotation.

fixSteps.title: Remove and Reseat Cartridges
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all toner cartridges and imaging drums. Check that nothing is binding in the cartridge areas. Reinstall everything.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle with Full Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the printer for 5 minutes. Plug into a different outlet (not a power strip) and restart.

fixSteps.title: Professional Motor Service
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor cannot start after clearing all obstructions, the motor assembly or drive train needs professional diagnosis and repair.

whenToCallPro: If the paper path is clear, cartridges are properly installed, and the motor still cannot start, professional service is required.

faq.q: Can cold temperatures cause 59.01?
faq.a: Yes, cold can increase the viscosity of lubricants in the motor and gear train, making startup harder. Allow the printer to warm to room temperature before use.

faq.q: Is 59.01 less serious than 59.00?
faq.a: Somewhat. 59.01 means the motor is trying but cannot reach speed, which may be fixable by clearing obstructions. 59.00 is a complete motor failure which almost always requires replacement.

faq.q: Can I hear the motor attempting to start?
faq.a: Often yes. With a 59.01 error, you may hear a brief motor whir or struggle followed by the error. Complete silence indicates a 59.00 type failure.

title: Main Motor Rotation Error
shortDescription: The main motor lost speed during operation.
fullDescription: Error 59.02 indicates the main motor was running but lost rotational speed during operation. This mid-operation failure suggests the motor encountered resistance (paper jam, worn gears) or the motor itself is degrading.

causes.cause: Paper jam creating resistance during printing
Worn drive gears causing increased friction
Motor overheating due to failed cooling

symptoms: 59.02 error during mid-print operation
Motor speed fluctuation heard during printing
Paper jams occurring simultaneously with error
Intermittent error during heavy print runs

fixSteps.title: Clear Any Jam
fixSteps.instruction: Check all paper paths and clear any jammed paper. The motor may have slowed due to paper resistance.

fixSteps.title: Check for Overheating
fixSteps.instruction: If the error occurs during long print runs, the motor may be overheating. Let the printer cool for 30 minutes and resume printing in smaller batches.

fixSteps.title: Reduce Print Volume
fixSteps.instruction: If the printer is exceeding its duty cycle, reduce print volume or allow rest periods between large jobs.

fixSteps.title: Professional Service
fixSteps.instruction: If the error recurs frequently, the motor or gear train needs professional inspection and potential replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the error occurs regularly without paper jams, the motor or drive train requires professional service.

faq.q: What is a duty cycle?
faq.a: The duty cycle is the maximum number of pages a printer is designed to handle per month. Exceeding it causes accelerated wear on motors, rollers, and other components.

faq.q: Can I check my printer's page count?
faq.a: Yes, print a configuration page from the control panel. It shows the total page count, which can be compared to the motor and maintenance kit rated lives.

faq.q: Does this error always mean the motor is failing?
faq.a: Not always. Paper jams are the most common cause of a 59.02. Only persistent errors after clearing jams indicate actual motor or mechanical problems.

title: Toner Cartridge Low
shortDescription: A toner cartridge is estimated to be running low on toner.
fullDescription: The Toner Low message is a pre-emptive warning that a toner cartridge is approaching its end of life. The printer will continue to print normally, but print quality may gradually degrade. This is a notification to order replacement toner, not an error that stops printing.

causes.cause: High coverage printing consuming toner faster

symptoms: Toner Low message on display or Embedded Web Server
Gradual fading of printed output
Status light amber or yellow
HP Smart app notification for low toner

fixSteps.title: Continue Printing
fixSteps.instruction: The printer will continue to print with Toner Low status. Print quality remains acceptable for some time after the message first appears.

fixSteps.title: Redistribute Toner
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the cartridge and gently rock it side to side 5-6 times. This redistributes remaining toner for more even coverage and may extend the cartridge life.

fixSteps.title: Order Replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Order a replacement cartridge so it is ready when quality becomes unacceptable. Check the printer label or HP Smart for the correct cartridge number.

fixSteps.title: Adjust Very Low Settings
fixSteps.instruction: Configure what happens when toner reaches Very Low. Go to Settings > Supply Settings > Black Cartridge > Very Low Setting. Choose between Continue, Stop, or Prompt.

whenToCallPro: No professional service needed. Toner replacement is a user task.

faq.q: How many more pages can I print after Toner Low?
faq.a: This varies by model and coverage, but typically you can print 200-500 more pages after the Toner Low message. The remaining yield depends on print coverage.

faq.q: Should I replace the cartridge immediately?
faq.a: No, continue printing until quality degrades. The Low warning appears well before the cartridge is empty, giving you time to order a replacement.

faq.q: Can I shake the cartridge to get more pages?
faq.a: Gently rocking the cartridge side to side can redistribute toner and extend life. Do not shake vigorously as this can cause toner spills.

title: Imaging Drum Nearing End of Life
shortDescription: The imaging drum is approaching its rated end of life.
fullDescription: The Imaging Drum Low message indicates the photosensitive imaging drum is nearing the end of its rated life. The drum is a separate component from the toner cartridge on some HP models and must be replaced periodically. Continuing to use a worn drum can cause print quality issues.

causes.cause: Drum has reached or is nearing rated page count
Heavy print volume accelerating drum wear
Drum surface scratched by foreign material

symptoms: Imaging Drum Low message on display
Faint repeating marks or spots on prints
Gradual quality degradation
Gray background on prints

fixSteps.title: Continue Printing
fixSteps.instruction: You can continue printing with a drum low warning. Quality will gradually degrade as the drum wears.

fixSteps.title: Order Replacement Drum
fixSteps.instruction: Order a replacement imaging drum for your model. Do not confuse with the toner cartridge which is a separate component.

fixSteps.title: Replace Imaging Drum
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover, remove the toner cartridge, then remove the imaging drum. Install the new drum and replace the toner cartridge on top of it.

fixSteps.title: Print Test Page
fixSteps.instruction: After drum replacement, print a test page to verify clean output with no repeating defects.

whenToCallPro: Imaging drum replacement is a simple user task. No professional service needed.

faq.q: What is the difference between the drum and toner cartridge?
faq.a: The toner cartridge contains the toner powder. The imaging drum is a photosensitive cylinder that receives the laser image and transfers toner to the paper. Some models integrate both into one unit.

faq.q: How long does an imaging drum last?
faq.a: HP imaging drums are typically rated for 12,000-50,000 pages depending on the model. Enterprise drums tend to have higher ratings.

faq.q: Can a worn drum damage my printer?
faq.a: No, a worn drum will not damage the printer, but it will produce increasingly poor print quality with streaks, spots, and gray backgrounds.

title: Network Connection Failed
shortDescription: The printer lost its network connection or cannot communicate on the network.
fullDescription: A Network Error indicates the printer has lost its wired or wireless network connection and cannot communicate with computers, servers, or cloud services. This prevents network printing, scanning to email/folder, and access to the Embedded Web Server.

causes.cause: Router or access point restarted or reconfigured
Wi-Fi signal interference or range issue
IP address conflict on the network

symptoms: Network error on printer display
Wi-Fi indicator light off or flashing
Cannot print from any computer on the network
Embedded Web Server inaccessible from browser

fixSteps.title: Check Physical Connection
fixSteps.instruction: For wired: verify the Ethernet cable is connected and the link light is active. For wireless: check the Wi-Fi indicator on the printer.

fixSteps.title: Restart Network Equipment
fixSteps.instruction: Restart your router, then restart the printer. Wait 2 minutes between each restart for the devices to fully initialize.

fixSteps.title: Reconnect Wireless
fixSteps.instruction: On the printer, go to Network Settings > Wireless Setup Wizard and reconnect to your Wi-Fi network.

fixSteps.title: Print Network Configuration Page
fixSteps.instruction: Print a network configuration page to verify the IP address, subnet mask, and gateway. Ensure these match your network settings.

whenToCallPro: If the printer's network hardware has failed (no Wi-Fi networks detected, Ethernet port shows no link light with a known-good cable).

faq.q: How far can my printer be from the router?
faq.a: For reliable Wi-Fi, keep the printer within 30 feet (10 meters) of the router with minimal walls in between. Thick walls, metal objects, and other electronics can reduce range.

faq.q: Should I use wired or wireless connection?
faq.a: Wired Ethernet is more reliable and faster. Use wireless only when running a cable is impractical. For enterprise printers handling heavy volume, wired is recommended.

faq.q: Can I use Wi-Fi Direct instead of network?
faq.a: Yes, Wi-Fi Direct allows direct device-to-printer connection without a router. However, the connected device cannot use its regular Wi-Fi simultaneously on some devices.

title: Printhead Not Detected or Failed
shortDescription: The printer cannot detect the printhead or it has failed.
fullDescription: The Printhead Missing or Failed error occurs on HP inkjet printers with a user-replaceable printhead (separate from the ink cartridges). The printer either cannot detect the printhead or the printhead has failed electrically. This prevents all printing operations.

causes.cause: Printhead not properly installed after cartridge change
Printhead electrical contacts dirty or corroded

symptoms: Printhead missing or failed error message
All printing functions disabled
Error persists after reinserting printhead
Printhead LED indicators not illuminating

fixSteps.title: Reseat the Printhead
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cartridge access door, remove all cartridges, then unlatch and remove the printhead. Wait 30 seconds, then reinstall the printhead firmly and replace all cartridges.

fixSteps.title: Clean Printhead Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the printhead and clean the electrical contacts on the printhead and inside the carriage with a lint-free cloth dampened with distilled water. Allow to dry completely.

fixSteps.title: Power Reset
fixSteps.instruction: With the printer on, disconnect the power cord from the back of the printer. Wait 60 seconds. Reconnect and power on.

fixSteps.title: Replace Printhead
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the printhead has failed and must be replaced. Order a replacement printhead for your specific model.

whenToCallPro: If a new printhead is also not detected, the carriage contact assembly may be damaged, which requires professional repair.

faq.q: How long does a printhead last?
faq.a: HP printheads are designed to last for the life of the printer under normal use. However, infrequent printing can cause nozzle clogging, and very heavy use can cause nozzle burnout.

faq.q: Can I clean a failed printhead?
faq.a: If the printhead has electrically failed (burned out nozzles), cleaning will not help. If it is clogged, soaking the nozzle plate in warm distilled water for 10-15 minutes may restore function.

faq.q: Is the printhead separate from the cartridges on all HP printers?
faq.a: No. Some HP printers (DeskJet, ENVY) integrate the printhead into the cartridge. Others (OfficeJet Pro) have a separate, user-replaceable printhead.

title: Printhead Alignment Failed
shortDescription: The printer failed to complete the printhead alignment process.
fullDescription: Alignment Failed occurs when the printer cannot complete the automatic printhead alignment calibration. Alignment ensures precise placement of ink drops for clear text and accurate colors. Failure may result in blurry text, misaligned colors, or banding in prints.

causes.cause: Alignment page printed on wrong paper type
Printhead nozzles clogged affecting alignment pattern
Ink cartridges very low on ink

symptoms: Alignment failed error message
Blurry or misaligned text on prints
Color banding or misregistration
Alignment process starts but does not complete

fixSteps.title: Use Correct Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Load plain white letter-size paper (not photo paper, labels, or cardstock) in the main tray. The alignment sensor requires plain paper for calibration.

fixSteps.title: Clean Printhead
fixSteps.instruction: Run the printhead cleaning utility from the printer control panel or HP Smart app. This clears clogged nozzles that could interfere with alignment.

fixSteps.title: Clean Scanner Glass
fixSteps.instruction: If your printer scans the alignment page, clean the scanner glass with a soft cloth and glass cleaner to ensure clear scanning.

fixSteps.title: Retry Alignment
fixSteps.instruction: From the printer menu or HP Smart, run the alignment process again. Ensure the alignment page prints clearly before the scanner reads it.

whenToCallPro: If alignment repeatedly fails after cleaning the printhead and scanner glass, the printhead or alignment sensor may be failing.

faq.q: Is alignment required after every cartridge change?
faq.a: The printer usually prompts for alignment after a new printhead or after certain cartridge changes. It is not always required but is recommended for optimal quality.

faq.q: Can I skip alignment?
faq.a: Yes, alignment can usually be skipped or canceled. However, print quality may suffer, especially for photos and graphics.

faq.q: Why does my printer keep asking for alignment?
faq.a: If the printer repeatedly requests alignment, the previous alignment may have failed silently or the alignment data was not saved properly. Complete the alignment process fully to resolve this.

title: Cartridge Installed in Wrong Slot
shortDescription: An ink or toner cartridge is installed in the incorrect color slot.
fullDescription: The Wrong Cartridge error indicates a cartridge has been installed in the wrong color slot. Each slot is designed for a specific color (Black, Cyan, Magenta, Yellow) and cartridges in the wrong position cannot function properly and may damage the printhead.

causes.cause: Cartridge placed in wrong color-coded slot
Color labels on cartridge or slot faded or unreadable

symptoms: Wrong cartridge error with color identified
Printer will not print after cartridge installation
Color-coded slot indicator showing mismatch
Incorrect colors in test print

fixSteps.title: Identify Correct Slots
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cartridge access area and check the color labels on each slot. Slots are labeled with the color name or a colored indicator.

fixSteps.title: Rearrange Cartridges
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all cartridges and reinstall each one in its matching color slot. Match the cartridge label to the slot label.

fixSteps.title: Verify Cartridge Numbers
fixSteps.instruction: Check that all installed cartridges are the correct part numbers for your printer model and are not from a different printer series.

fixSteps.title: Close and Test
fixSteps.instruction: Close the cartridge door and wait for the printer to initialize. Print a test page to verify correct color output.

whenToCallPro: This does not require professional service. It is a user installation error.

faq.q: Can putting a cartridge in the wrong slot damage my printer?
faq.a: On most modern HP printers, the cartridge will not fit properly or the printer will detect the error before damage occurs. However, forcing a cartridge into the wrong slot could damage the contacts.

faq.q: How do I tell which cartridge goes where?
faq.a: Each cartridge has a color label or code. The printer slots are marked with matching colors. Some printers also have shape-coded slots that only accept the correct cartridge.

faq.q: What if all cartridges look the same?
faq.a: Check the cartridge number printed on each cartridge. Each color has a unique number. If the physical labels have faded, the number identifies the color.

title: Printhead Problem (Inkjet)
shortDescription: A problem has been detected with the printhead.
fullDescription: Error 0xc19a0003 indicates a critical printhead problem on HP inkjet printers. The printer detected an issue with the printhead that prevents normal operation. This can be caused by electrical failure, severe clogging, or contact problems.

causes.cause: Printhead electrical contacts dirty or corroded
Severe printhead nozzle clogging from infrequent use

symptoms: Error 0xc19a0003 on display or HP Smart
All printing functions blocked
Printhead cleaning cycles fail
Error appears immediately after printer initialization

fixSteps.title: Hard Power Reset
fixSteps.instruction: With the printer on, pull the power cord from the back of the printer. Wait 2 minutes. Reconnect and power on. This resets the printhead controller.

fixSteps.title: Clean All Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove cartridges and printhead. Clean all electrical contacts with a lint-free cloth and distilled water. Let dry completely before reassembly.

fixSteps.title: Deep Clean Printhead
fixSteps.instruction: Soak the printhead nozzle plate in 1/4 inch of warm distilled water for 10 minutes to dissolve dried ink. Blot dry with a paper towel.

fixSteps.title: Replace Printhead
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after thorough cleaning, the printhead has failed and must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists with a new printhead, the carriage electronics may be damaged.

faq.q: Why did my printhead fail?
faq.a: Common causes include infrequent printing (ink dries in nozzles), using non-HP ink that damages nozzles, power surges affecting electronics, or simply reaching end of life after heavy use.

faq.q: How much does a replacement printhead cost?
faq.a: HP replacement printheads typically cost $40-$80 depending on the model. This is significantly less than a new printer.

faq.q: How do I prevent printhead failure?
faq.a: Print at least one page per week to keep nozzles clear. Use HP-original ink cartridges. Connect the printer to a surge protector.

title: Printer Cover or Door Open (Inkjet)
shortDescription: A cover or door on the inkjet printer is open or not latched.
fullDescription: The Cover Open message on HP inkjet printers indicates the cartridge access door, scanner lid, or another cover is not fully closed. The printer uses magnetic or mechanical sensors to detect door status and halts all operations when a cover is open.

causes.cause: Cartridge access door not fully closed
Cartridge not fully seated preventing door closure

symptoms: Cover open message on display
Printer will not print or scan
Carriage moves to access position when door opens
Door appears closed but sensor not triggered

fixSteps.title: Close All Covers
fixSteps.instruction: Firmly close the cartridge access door, scanner lid, and any other doors. Push until you hear a click.

fixSteps.title: Check Cartridge Seating
fixSteps.instruction: If the cartridge door will not close, open it and press each cartridge down until it clicks. A raised cartridge can prevent door closure.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Power off, wait 30 seconds, power on. This resets the door sensors.

fixSteps.title: Check Door Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists with the door closed, the magnetic sensor may be misaligned. Check for a small magnet on the door that aligns with a sensor on the printer body.

whenToCallPro: Only if the door sensor is physically broken or the door latch mechanism is damaged.

faq.q: Can a broken door hinge cause this?
faq.a: Yes, if the hinge is damaged and the door cannot close completely, the sensor will not be triggered. The hinge may need repair or replacement.

faq.q: My scanner lid is not flat. Does that matter?
faq.a: The scanner lid usually does not trigger a cover open error that blocks printing. However, on some MFP models, a not-fully-closed scanner lid can trigger the error.

faq.q: Why does the carriage move when I open the door?
faq.a: This is normal. The printer moves the carriage to the access position so you can reach the cartridges. It returns to the home position when the door is closed.

title: Imaging Drum End of Life
shortDescription: The imaging drum has reached end of life and must be replaced.
fullDescription: The imaging drum has exceeded its rated page count and is no longer producing acceptable print quality. The drum must be replaced for the printer to continue producing clear, defect-free output.

causes.cause: Drum surface physically damaged by foreign material
Heavy print volume accelerating drum wear
Drum counter chip indicating end of life

symptoms: Replace Drum message on display
Repeating marks or spots on every page
Gray or faded background on prints
Vertical streaks on output

fixSteps.title: Order Replacement Drum
fixSteps.instruction: Order the correct imaging drum for your printer model.

fixSteps.title: Remove Old Drum
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front cover, remove the toner cartridge, then remove the imaging drum unit.

fixSteps.title: Install New Drum
fixSteps.instruction: Unbox the new drum, insert the toner cartridge into it, and install the assembly into the printer.

fixSteps.title: Reset Drum Counter
fixSteps.instruction: The printer may automatically detect the new drum, or you may need to reset the counter from the supplies menu.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Drum replacement is a user task.

faq.q: How long does a drum last?
faq.a: HP imaging drums are typically rated for 12,000-50,000 pages depending on the model.

faq.q: Is the drum the same as the toner cartridge?
faq.a: No, the drum is separate. The toner sits on or in the drum unit. Some models integrate both.

faq.q: Can a worn drum damage the printer?
faq.a: No, but it will produce increasingly poor print quality.

title: Carriage Stall Error
shortDescription: The printer carriage has stalled and cannot move freely.
fullDescription:
Error 0x6100004a indicates the printhead carriage is stuck or cannot move along its track. This is commonly caused by a paper jam, packing material left inside the printer, or a foreign object blocking the carriage path.

The carriage must move freely from side to side for the printer to operate. Obstructions, dried ink buildup on the carriage rail, or a failed carriage motor can all trigger this error.

In some cases, the encoder strip that tracks carriage position may be dirty or damaged, causing the printer to incorrectly detect a stall condition.

causes.cause: Paper jam or torn paper blocking carriage path
Packing material or tape not removed
Dried ink buildup on carriage rail

symptoms: Printer displays 0x6100004a error
Carriage does not move during startup
Grinding or clicking sounds from carriage area
Print jobs fail immediately

fixSteps.title: Power Off and Open Printer
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer and unplug it. Open the ink cartridge access door and look for any obstructions in the carriage path.

fixSteps.title: Remove Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove any jammed paper, packing tape, or foreign objects blocking the carriage. Gently slide the carriage left and right by hand to verify it moves freely.

fixSteps.title: Clean Carriage Rail and Encoder Strip
fixSteps.instruction: Use a lint-free cloth lightly dampened with distilled water to clean the metal carriage rail and the clear plastic encoder strip running behind the carriage.

fixSteps.title: Reset Printer
fixSteps.instruction: Reconnect power, turn on the printer, and allow it to initialize. The carriage should move freely during startup.

whenToCallPro: If the carriage motor is burned out or the encoder strip is physically broken and needs replacement.

faq.q: Can I move the carriage by hand?
faq.a: Yes, with the printer unplugged you can gently slide the carriage left and right to check for obstructions.

faq.q: What is the encoder strip?
faq.a: It is a thin clear plastic strip with fine lines that runs horizontally behind the carriage. The printer reads it to track carriage position.

title: Printhead Ink System Failure
shortDescription: The ink delivery system has failed, preventing the printhead from receiving ink properly.
fullDescription:
Error 0xc19a0020 indicates a failure in the ink delivery system between the ink cartridges and the printhead. The printer detects that ink is not flowing correctly to the printhead nozzles.

This error is common on HP printers with integrated ink systems where the cartridge and printhead are separate components. Clogged ink channels, air bubbles in the ink lines, or a failed printhead can trigger this code.

Attempting to print with this error active may result in blank pages, streaked output, or further damage to the printhead assembly.

causes.cause: Air bubbles in ink delivery system

symptoms: Error 0xc19a0020 on display or computer
Blank or streaked prints
Ink cartridges appear full but no ink reaches paper
Printhead cleaning cycles fail

fixSteps.title: Remove and Reseat Cartridges
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cartridge access door, remove all ink cartridges, wait 30 seconds, then reinstall them firmly until they click.

fixSteps.title: Run Printhead Cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: Go to printer settings or HP Smart app and run the printhead cleaning utility. Run it up to 3 times, printing a test page between each cycle.

fixSteps.title: Manual Printhead Cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the printhead and soak the bottom nozzle plate in warm distilled water for 10 minutes. Blot dry with lint-free cloth and reinstall.

fixSteps.title: Replace Printhead
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve the issue, purchase a replacement printhead for your model from HP or authorized retailers. Install following the printer manual.

whenToCallPro: If printhead replacement does not resolve the error, the printer may have an internal ink delivery system defect.

faq.q: Can I use third-party ink cartridges?
faq.a: Third-party cartridges can sometimes cause ink system failures. Try using genuine HP cartridges to rule out cartridge quality issues.

faq.q: How much does a replacement printhead cost?
faq.a: HP printheads typically cost between $30-80 depending on the model. Check HP's parts store for your specific printer.

title: Critical Firmware Error
shortDescription: A critical firmware error has caused the printer to halt and require a restart.
fullDescription:
The 79.XXXX Service Error is a critical firmware-level error on HP LaserJet printers that causes the printer to stop responding. The XXXX portion represents a specific sub-code identifying the type of firmware failure.

This error is often triggered by corrupt print jobs, incompatible PCL or PostScript commands, firmware bugs, or corrupted NVRAM. Network-related issues such as malformed SNMP packets can also cause this error on networked printers.

The error is similar to a computer crash and usually requires a power cycle to clear. If it recurs, the underlying cause must be addressed to prevent repeated failures.
symptoms: 79.XXXX Service Error on control panel
Printer unresponsive to all commands
Printer stuck in restart loop
Error appears during specific print jobs

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle Printer
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug the power cable, wait 30 seconds, then reconnect and power on.

fixSteps.title: Clear Print Queue
fixSteps.instruction: On the computer, open the print queue for this printer and cancel all pending jobs. Disconnect the network cable before powering on the printer to prevent new jobs.

fixSteps.title: Update Firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Download the latest firmware for your printer model from HP's support website. Install it via USB connection following HP's firmware update instructions.

fixSteps.title: Remove DIMM/EIO Cards
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, power off and remove any third-party DIMM memory modules or EIO cards. Power on to test if the error clears.

whenToCallPro: If the 79 error recurs after firmware update and clearing all print jobs, the formatter board may need replacement.

faq.q: Is this the same as error 49?
faq.a: Similar but different. Error 49 is a general firmware error while 79 specifically indicates a service-level critical failure that usually requires a power cycle.

faq.q: Can a single print job cause this?
faq.a: Yes, a corrupt PDF or a document with unsupported PostScript commands can trigger a 79 error. Try printing from a different application.

title: Paper Jam in Tray 2 Pickup Area
shortDescription: Paper has jammed in the Tray 2 pickup area and must be removed.
fullDescription:
Error 13.05.00 indicates a paper jam in the Tray 2 area of an HP LaserJet printer. The paper has either failed to feed from Tray 2 or has misfed and become stuck in the pickup roller area.

This jam type often occurs when paper is loaded incorrectly, the tray guides are not adjusted properly, or the pickup roller in Tray 2 has become worn. Humid or curled paper can also contribute to misfeed jams.

Repeated 13.05.00 jams usually indicate a worn pickup roller assembly that needs cleaning or replacement.

causes.cause: Paper guides not adjusted to paper size

symptoms: 13.05.00 error on control panel
Paper does not feed from Tray 2
Partial sheet visible in Tray 2 area
Multiple sheets feeding simultaneously

fixSteps.title: Remove Tray 2 and Clear Jam
fixSteps.instruction: Pull out Tray 2 completely. Remove any jammed or wrinkled paper from the tray opening and from inside the tray itself.

fixSteps.title: Inspect and Reload Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Fan the paper stack, ensure it is not curled or damp. Reload the tray and adjust the side and rear guides so they just touch the paper edges without bending them.

fixSteps.title: Clean Pickup Roller
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the pickup roller from Tray 2 by releasing the locking tabs. Clean the rubber surface with a lint-free cloth dampened with water. Allow to dry and reinstall.

fixSteps.title: Replace Pickup Roller
fixSteps.instruction: If the roller is smooth, cracked, or visibly worn, replace it with a new pickup roller kit designed for your printer model.

whenToCallPro: If pickup roller and separation pad replacement does not resolve recurring jams, internal feed assembly damage may require professional service.

faq.q: Why does only Tray 2 jam?
faq.a: Tray 2 has its own pickup roller and separation pad. These wear independently from Tray 1 components and may need replacement at different intervals.

faq.q: Can I use Tray 1 while Tray 2 is jamming?
faq.a: Yes, you can set Tray 1 as the default paper source in printer settings while troubleshooting Tray 2.

title: Paper Jam in Fuser Area
shortDescription: Paper is jammed inside or near the fuser assembly.
fullDescription:
Error 13.20.00 reports a paper jam inside or around the fuser unit. The fuser is the heated component that bonds toner to paper. Paper getting stuck in this area can be caused by wrinkled or damaged paper, worn fuser rollers, or incorrect paper type settings.

Since the fuser operates at high temperatures (around 200°C/392°F), always allow the printer to cool for several minutes before attempting to remove jammed paper from this area to avoid burns.

Repeated fuser jams may indicate the fuser assembly is reaching end of life and the rollers or pressure pads need replacement.

causes.cause: Paper wrinkled or folded before entering fuser
Wrong paper type or weight for fuser settings
Worn fuser rollers or pressure pads
Fuser assembly reaching end of life

symptoms: 13.20.00 error on display
Paper stuck near rear output area
Partially fused prints
Warm or hot paper with unfixed toner

fixSteps.title: Power Off and Cool Down
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer and unplug it. Wait at least 10 minutes for the fuser to cool before opening the rear access door.

fixSteps.title: Remove Jammed Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear access door or top cover to access the fuser area. Gently pull the jammed paper out in the direction of paper travel. Do not tear the paper.

fixSteps.title: Check Paper Settings
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the paper type and weight settings in the printer driver match the actual paper loaded. Heavy paper requires different fuser temperature settings.

fixSteps.title: Replace Fuser Assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If jams recur, purchase a replacement fuser assembly for your model. Remove the old fuser by releasing the locking levers and sliding it out, then install the new one.

whenToCallPro: If the fuser assembly needs replacement or internal fuser components are damaged beyond user-serviceable repair.

faq.q: Is it safe to touch the fuser?
faq.a: No. The fuser reaches very high temperatures. Always wait at least 10 minutes after powering off before touching the fuser area.

faq.q: How long does a fuser last?
faq.a: Typically 100,000-200,000 pages depending on the model. Check the supplies status page for remaining fuser life.

title: Ink System Service Station Error
shortDescription: The ink system service station mechanism has failed or is stuck.
fullDescription:
Error 0x610000f6 indicates a failure in the ink system service station, which is the mechanism that caps, cleans, and primes the printhead when the printer is idle or performing maintenance cycles.

The service station includes a rubber capping pad, a wiper blade, and a spit pad. When this mechanism jams or its motor fails, the printer cannot perform essential printhead maintenance, leading to this error.

This error may appear after a paper jam in the service station area, after dried ink accumulates on the capping mechanism, or when the service station motor reaches end of life.

causes.cause: Dried ink clogging service station mechanism
Paper or debris jammed in service station
Wiper blade or capping pad worn

symptoms: 0x610000f6 error on screen
Printhead cannot return to home position
Ink smears or streaks on prints
Printer fails during startup maintenance

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug for 5 minutes. Plug back in and power on to reset the service station sequence.

fixSteps.title: Clear Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the printer and gently slide the printhead carriage to the left to expose the service station on the right side. Remove any visible paper or debris.

fixSteps.title: Clean Service Station
fixSteps.instruction: Use a lint-free cloth dampened with warm distilled water to clean the rubber capping pad and wiper blade. Remove any dried ink buildup.

fixSteps.title: Test Service Station Movement
fixSteps.instruction: With the printer plugged in, observe whether the service station cap moves up and down during startup. If it does not move, the motor may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the service station motor is burned out or the mechanism is mechanically broken beyond cleaning.

faq.q: What does the service station do?
faq.a: It caps the printhead when not in use to prevent ink from drying, wipes the nozzle plate to keep it clean, and primes the ink system during maintenance cycles.

faq.q: Can I replace the service station myself?
faq.a: On some HP models the service station is user-replaceable, but on many it requires significant disassembly. Check your model's service manual.

title: Fuser Inconsistent Error
shortDescription: The fuser is not reaching or maintaining the correct operating temperature.
fullDescription:
Error 50.5 indicates the fuser assembly is experiencing temperature inconsistencies. The fuser is unable to maintain stable operating temperature, which can result in poor print quality or complete printing failure.

This error typically indicates a failing fuser lamp, a malfunctioning thermistor sensor, or a problem with the power supply to the fuser. The fuser must maintain precise temperatures to properly bond toner to paper.

On color LaserJet models, 50.5 may indicate issues with a specific color fuser station or the intermediate transfer belt heating element.

causes.cause: Fuser lamp or heating element degradation

symptoms: 50.5 Fuser Error on display
Toner not bonding to paper properly
Smeared or easily smudged prints
Printer shuts down during warm-up

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug for 2 minutes, and plug back in. This may reset a temporary fuser sensor glitch.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Fuser Assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear door, release the fuser retention clips, remove the fuser, and firmly reinstall it ensuring it is fully seated.

fixSteps.title: Check Fuser Connections
fixSteps.instruction: With the printer unplugged, inspect the fuser cable connector for bent pins or loose connections. Firmly reseat the connector.

fixSteps.title: Replace Fuser Assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists, the fuser heating element or thermistor is likely failed. Replace the entire fuser assembly with one rated for your model.

whenToCallPro: If the fuser and power supply both need diagnosis, as specialized equipment is needed to test internal voltages.

faq.q: Are all 50.x errors the same?
faq.a: No. 50.1 is low fuser temp, 50.2 is slow warm-up, 50.3 is high temp, 50.4 is sensor failure, and 50.5 is temperature inconsistency. Each has different root causes.

faq.q: Can I use a refurbished fuser?
faq.a: Yes, refurbished fuser assemblies are available at lower cost. Ensure they are rated for your specific printer model.

title: Drum Motor Startup Error
shortDescription: The drum motor failed to reach proper speed during startup.
fullDescription:
Error 59.04 indicates the drum motor (also known as the main motor) failed to reach its required operating speed during the printer startup sequence. The drum motor drives the photoconductor drum, transfer belt, and paper feed mechanisms.

This error is commonly caused by a mechanical obstruction preventing the motor from spinning freely, a failing motor, or a defective motor controller on the DC controller board.

Foreign objects such as paper clips, staples, or torn paper fragments can get lodged in the drive gear train and stall the motor.
symptoms: 59.04 error on control panel
Motor does not spin at startup
Grinding noise during startup
Printer fails to initialize

fixSteps.title: Remove Toner Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front door and remove the toner cartridge. Check for any visible obstructions or debris inside the cartridge bay.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the printer for paper clips, staples, or torn paper. Rotate the drum gears by hand to check for binding or resistance.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Toner Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Reinstall the toner cartridge firmly, ensuring it clicks into place. Close the front door and attempt to power on.

fixSteps.title: Test with New Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: If available, try a different toner cartridge. A damaged cartridge gear can prevent the drum motor from spinning.

whenToCallPro: If the motor or DC controller board is defective and needs replacement.

faq.q: Can a bad toner cartridge cause this?
faq.a: Yes, a cartridge with a damaged drive gear can prevent the motor from spinning and trigger a 59.04 error.

faq.q: How much does a motor replacement cost?
faq.a: The drum motor itself is typically $30-60, but labor for installation can bring the total to $150-300.

title: Toner Supply Memory Error
shortDescription: The printer cannot read the memory chip on the toner cartridge.
fullDescription:
Error 10.10.00 indicates the printer is unable to communicate with the memory chip (e-label) on the toner cartridge installed in the black position. This chip stores cartridge information including toner level, page count, and regional data.

The error is commonly caused by a dirty or damaged chip contact, a defective cartridge memory chip, or a non-genuine cartridge with an incompatible chip. In some cases, a firmware update may have changed chip compatibility requirements.

The printer may still allow printing with this error, but toner level reporting will be inaccurate and the printer may display supply warnings.

causes.cause: Dirty contacts on cartridge or printer

symptoms: 10.10.00 error on display
Toner level shows unknown
Supply status warning
Printer may refuse to print

fixSteps.title: Remove and Reseat Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the toner cartridge, inspect the gold or copper contacts on the chip area. Clean with a dry lint-free cloth. Reinstall firmly.

fixSteps.title: Clean Printer Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: With the cartridge removed, locate the spring-loaded contacts inside the cartridge bay. Gently clean them with a dry lint-free cloth.

fixSteps.title: Try a Different Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Install a known-good HP genuine cartridge to determine if the issue is with the specific cartridge or the printer.

fixSteps.title: Override Supply Error
fixSteps.instruction: On the control panel, navigate to Settings > Supply Settings > Black Cartridge and select 'Continue' to override the supply error if the cartridge is known good.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists with multiple genuine HP cartridges, the internal contact assembly may need replacement.

faq.q: Can I use non-HP cartridges?
faq.a: HP printers may reject or show errors with non-genuine cartridges. Using HP genuine cartridges eliminates chip compatibility issues.

faq.q: Will the printer work with this error?
faq.a: Some models allow you to override the error and continue printing, but toner level monitoring will not function.

title: Cyan Toner Supply Memory Error
shortDescription: The printer cannot read the memory chip on the cyan toner cartridge.
fullDescription:
Error 10.22.00 indicates a communication failure with the memory chip on the cyan toner cartridge. This error is specific to color HP LaserJet printers and affects only the cyan color channel.

The memory chip on each color cartridge stores calibration data, toner level, and usage information. When the printer cannot read this chip, it may disable printing to prevent damage or continue with degraded supply monitoring.

This is one of several supply errors in the 10.XX.XX family, where the second digit indicates the cartridge position (10 = black, 22 = cyan, 32 = magenta, 42 = yellow).

causes.cause: Dirty contacts on cyan cartridge chip

symptoms: 10.22.00 error on display
Cyan toner level shows unknown
Color prints have missing cyan
Printer may stop all color printing

fixSteps.title: Reseat Cyan Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Open the front door, remove the cyan toner cartridge, and reinstall it firmly. Ensure it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Clean Chip Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the cyan cartridge and gently clean the gold memory chip contacts with a dry lint-free cloth. Also clean the corresponding contacts inside the printer.

fixSteps.title: Replace Cyan Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve the error, replace the cyan cartridge with a genuine HP cartridge.

fixSteps.title: Print in Black Only
fixSteps.instruction: As a workaround, set the printer to black-only mode in printer properties to continue printing while resolving the cyan cartridge issue.

affectedSeries: HP Color LaserJet Pro MFP M479
HP Color LaserJet Enterprise MFP M480
whenToCallPro: If multiple cyan cartridges show the same error, the printer's internal contact pins for the cyan position may be bent or damaged.

faq.q: What do the numbers in 10.22.00 mean?
faq.a: The 10 is the supply error category, 22 identifies the cyan cartridge position, and 00 is the specific error type. Black is 10, cyan is 22, magenta is 32, yellow is 42.

faq.q: Can I print in black and white with this error?
faq.a: Most HP color LaserJets allow you to switch to black-only printing mode while a color cartridge has an error.

title: Paper Jam in Tray 1 (Multipurpose Tray)
shortDescription: Paper has jammed in the Tray 1 or multipurpose tray area.
fullDescription:
Error 13.03.00 indicates a paper jam originating from Tray 1, also known as the multipurpose tray or manual feed tray. This tray is typically the fold-down tray on the front of the printer used for special media or manual feeding.

Jams in Tray 1 often occur because the tray guides are not adjusted to the paper width, the paper is too thick or curled for the feed mechanism, or too many sheets are loaded in the priority tray.

Since Tray 1 is designed for smaller quantities and special media, it is more sensitive to paper quality and loading technique than the main paper cassette.

causes.cause: Paper guides not adjusted to paper width

symptoms: 13.03.00 error on display
Paper stuck in front tray area
Paper skews entering printer from Tray 1
Manual feed tray does not pick paper

fixSteps.title: Remove Jammed Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Gently pull any visible jammed paper from the Tray 1 area. Pull slowly and evenly to avoid tearing.

fixSteps.title: Adjust Paper Guides
fixSteps.instruction: Reload paper into Tray 1 and slide the paper width guides so they touch the edges of the paper without bending it.

fixSteps.title: Reduce Paper Quantity
fixSteps.instruction: Tray 1 typically holds 50-100 sheets maximum. Remove excess paper and ensure the stack does not exceed the maximum fill line.

fixSteps.title: Use Fresh Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Replace curled, wrinkled, or damp paper with fresh sheets from a sealed ream. Ensure paper meets printer specifications.

whenToCallPro: If jams persist after verifying paper quality and guide alignment, the Tray 1 pickup assembly may need professional replacement.

faq.q: How many sheets can Tray 1 hold?
faq.a: Typically 50-100 sheets of standard 75-80 g/m2 paper, depending on the model. Check your printer manual for the exact capacity.

faq.q: Can I use envelopes in Tray 1?
faq.a: Yes, Tray 1 is the recommended tray for envelopes. Load them flap-down with the short edge entering first, and adjust the guides.

title: Paper Jam in Duplex Unit
shortDescription: Paper has jammed inside the automatic duplexing (double-sided printing) unit.
fullDescription:
Error 13.12.00 indicates a paper jam inside the automatic duplex unit. During double-sided printing, the paper must reverse direction and re-enter the printer for the second side. Jams occur when the paper fails to navigate this reversal path.

Common causes include paper that is too light, too heavy, or too small for duplexing, as well as mechanical issues with the duplex unit's internal rollers. Humidity and curled paper can also cause problems during the reversal process.

Not all paper sizes and types support duplexing. Check your printer specifications for supported duplex media types.

causes.cause: Paper type or size not supported for duplexing
Duplex unit rollers worn or dirty

symptoms: 13.12.00 error during duplex printing
Paper stuck in rear duplex area
Second side of page is blank or missing
Duplex printing always jams

fixSteps.title: Access Duplex Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Open the rear door or remove the duplex unit (usually accessible from the rear of the printer). Remove any jammed paper.

fixSteps.title: Reseat Duplex Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the duplex unit completely, check for any paper remnants, and firmly reinstall it until it clicks into place.

fixSteps.title: Verify Paper Compatibility
fixSteps.instruction: Check printer specifications for supported duplex paper sizes (typically Letter/A4) and weights (typically 60-120 g/m2). Use appropriate paper.

fixSteps.title: Clean Duplex Rollers
